steps.title: Use the discriminant test
steps.content: For a quadratic $ax^2+bx+c=0$, no real solution means the discriminant is negative: $b^2-4ac\lt0$. Here $a=-2$ and $c=-32$, so $$b^2-4(-2)(-32)\lt0.$$

steps.title: Find the allowable values of b
steps.content: Simplify the inequality: $$b^2-256\lt0$$ $$b^2\lt256.$$ Thus $-16\lt b\lt16$. Since the question says $b$ is a positive integer, the possible values are $1$ through $15$.

steps.title: Pick the largest integer
steps.content: The largest positive integer less than $16$ is $15$. At $b=16$, the discriminant is $0$, which would give one real solution instead of no real solution.

steps.title: Check the boundary
steps.content: With $b=15$, the discriminant is $15^2-256=-31$, which is negative, so the equation has no real solution. Therefore the greatest possible value is 15.
